message:
Update CI workflows for tenzir-changelog

- Simplify docs.yaml: remove changelog reminder logic, keep docs preview
- Update release.yaml: use tenzir-changelog with --title and --intro
- Add pre-check for unreleased entries before creating releases
- Restore release titles to all 164 manifest.yaml files
- Remove empty intro fields from manifests

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -5,8 +5,8 @@ on:
     types: [opened, synchronize, reopened]
 
 jobs:
-  add-comment-and-build-docs:
-    name: Add Comment and Build Docs Preview
+  build-docs:
+    name: Build Docs Preview
     runs-on: ubuntu-latest
     permissions:
       pull-requests: write
@@ -15,117 +15,6 @@ jobs:
     steps:
       - name: Checkout repository
         uses: actions/checkout@v4

-          BUGFIX_URL=$(python3 changelog/add.py bugfix --web --pr "$PR_NUMBER" --branch "$BRANCH")

-          if [ "$HAS_CHANGELOG" = "true" ]; then

-          > [!NOTE]

-          >
-          > If you need to add additional changelog entries, you can use the links below or run \`./changelog/add.py change|bugfix|feature\` on the command-line.
-
-          ## 📝 Changelog Entries

-          |------|-------------|------|

-          | 🐛 **Bugfix** | Fixes for bugs or issues | [Add Bugfix](${{ steps.check-and-generate.outputs.bugfix_url }}) |
-          | ✨ **Feature** | New functionality or enhancements | [Add Feature](${{ steps.check-and-generate.outputs.feature_url }}) |
-
-          ## 📚 Documentation Preview
-
-          🔄 **Building docs preview...** This may take a few minutes.

-          else
-            COMMENT_BODY="<!-- add-changelog-entry -->
-          > [!TIP]

-          >
-          > You can also add a changelog entry manually by running \`./changelog/add.py change|bugfix|feature\` on the command-line.
-
-          ## 📝 Changelog Entries
-
-          | Type | Description | Link |
-          |------|-------------|------|
-          | 🔄 **Change** | Modifications to existing functionality | [Add Change](${{ steps.check-and-generate.outputs.change_url }}) |
-          | 🐛 **Bugfix** | Fixes for bugs or issues | [Add Bugfix](${{ steps.check-and-generate.outputs.bugfix_url }}) |
-          | ✨ **Feature** | New functionality or enhancements | [Add Feature](${{ steps.check-and-generate.outputs.feature_url }}) |
-
-          ## 📚 Documentation Preview
-
-          🔄 **Building docs preview...** This may take a few minutes.
-
-
-          *This section will be updated when the build completes.*"
-          fi
-
-          # Check if a comment already exists
-          COMMENT_ID=$(gh api repos/${{ github.repository }}/issues/$PR_NUMBER/comments \
-            --jq '.[] | select(.body | startswith("<!-- add-changelog-entry -->")) | .id')
-
-          if [ -n "$COMMENT_ID" ]; then
-            # Update existing comment
-            echo "Updating existing comment $COMMENT_ID"
-            gh api repos/${{ github.repository }}/issues/comments/$COMMENT_ID \

-          else
-            # Create new comment
-            echo "Creating new comment"
-            gh api repos/${{ github.repository }}/issues/$PR_NUMBER/comments \
-              -f body="$COMMENT_BODY"
-          fi
 
       - name: Checkout docs repository
         uses: actions/checkout@v4
@@ -152,14 +41,12 @@ jobs:
           cd docs-repo

-      - name: Update changelog and reference

         run: |

           rsync -av --delete ./docs/functions/ ./docs-repo/src/content/docs/reference/functions
           rsync -av --delete ./docs/operators/ ./docs-repo/src/content/docs/reference/operators
           cp ./docs/openapi.node.yaml ./docs-repo/src/content/apis/openapi.node.yaml
           cp ./tenzir.yaml.example ./docs-repo/tenzir.yaml.example
-          # Generate complete reference documentation (overviews and sidebar)
           cd docs-repo
           pnpm run generate:reference
 
@@ -179,56 +66,40 @@ jobs:
           surge ./dist https://tenzir-tenzir-preview-${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}.surge.sh --token ${{ secrets.SURGE_TOKEN }}
         continue-on-error: true
 
-      - name: Update comment with docs preview
+      - name: Post docs preview comment
         env:
           GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
         run: |
           PR_NUMBER="${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}"
-          HAS_CHANGELOG="${{ steps.check-and-generate.outputs.has_changelog_entries }}"

-          $docs_content"

-
-          # Get existing comment

-
-          if [ -n "$COMMENT_ID" ]; then
-            EXISTING_COMMENT=$(gh api repos/${{ github.repository }}/issues/comments/$COMMENT_ID \
-              --jq '.body')
-
-            if [ "${{ steps.build.outcome }}" = "success" ] && [ "${{ steps.deploy.outcome }}" = "success" ]; then
-              # Success case
-              PREVIEW_URL="https://tenzir-tenzir-preview-$PR_NUMBER.surge.sh"
-              DOCS_CONTENT="✅ **Docs preview is ready!**
+          ✅ **Docs preview is ready!**
 
-          🔗 **Preview Docs**: [$PREVIEW_URL]($PREVIEW_URL)
+          🔗 **Preview**: [$PREVIEW_URL]($PREVIEW_URL)
 
-          *The preview will be updated automatically when you push new commits to this PR.*"
-            else
-              # Failure case
-              DOCS_CONTENT="❌ **Docs preview build failed**
+          *Updated automatically when you push new commits.*"
+          else
+            COMMENT_BODY="<!-- docs-preview -->
+          ## 📚 Documentation Preview
 
-          The documentation preview could not be built or deployed. This might be a temporary issue.
+          ❌ **Docs preview build failed**
 
-          You can trigger a rebuild by pushing a new commit or closing and reopening this PR."
-            fi
+          Check the workflow logs for details."
+          fi
 
-            UPDATED_COMMENT=$(update_docs_section "$EXISTING_COMMENT" "$DOCS_CONTENT")
+          # Check if a comment already exists
+          COMMENT_ID=$(gh api repos/${{ github.repository }}/issues/$PR_NUMBER/comments \
+            --jq '.[] | select(.body | startswith("<!-- docs-preview -->")) | .id' | head -1)
 
+          if [ -n "$COMMENT_ID" ]; then
             gh api repos/${{ github.repository }}/issues/comments/$COMMENT_ID \
               -X PATCH \
-              -f body="$UPDATED_COMMENT"
+              -f body="$COMMENT_BODY"
+          else
+            gh api repos/${{ github.repository }}/issues/$PR_NUMBER/comments \
+              -f body="$COMMENT_BODY"
           fi

@@ -96,23 +96,33 @@ jobs:
 
       - name: Check if release already exists
         run: |
-          if [ -f "changelog/releases/${{ github.event.inputs.version }}.yaml" ]; then
-            echo "Error: Release file for ${{ github.event.inputs.version }} already exists"
+          if [ -d "changelog/releases/${{ github.event.inputs.version }}" ]; then
+            echo "Error: Release directory for ${{ github.event.inputs.version }} already exists"
             exit 1
           fi
           if git tag -l | grep -q "^${{ github.event.inputs.version }}$"; then
             echo "Error: Tag ${{ github.event.inputs.version }} already exists"
             exit 1
           fi
 
-      - name: Run release script
+      - name: Check for unreleased entries
+        run: |
+          if [ ! -d "changelog/unreleased" ] || [ -z "$(ls -A changelog/unreleased 2>/dev/null)" ]; then
+            echo "Error: No unreleased changelog entries found in changelog/unreleased/"
+            echo "Add changelog entries before creating a release."
+            exit 1
+          fi
+          echo "Found $(ls changelog/unreleased | wc -l | tr -d ' ') unreleased entries"
+
+      - name: Create release with tenzir-changelog
         env:
           RELEASE_TITLE: ${{ github.event.inputs.title }}
           RELEASE_DESCRIPTION: ${{ github.event.inputs.description }}
         run: |
-          ./changelog/release.py "${{ github.event.inputs.version }}" \
+          uvx tenzir-changelog release create "${{ github.event.inputs.version }}" \
             --title "$RELEASE_TITLE" \
-            --description "$RELEASE_DESCRIPTION"
+            --intro "$RELEASE_DESCRIPTION" \
+            --yes
 
       - name: Update version.json and Python package versions
         run: |
@@ -138,15 +148,19 @@ jobs:
           echo "Updated version.json with tenzir-version: ${VERSION_NUMBER}"
           echo "Updated Python package versions to: ${VERSION_NUMBER}"
 
-      - name: Verify release file was created
+      - name: Verify release was created
         run: |
-          if [ ! -f "changelog/releases/${{ github.event.inputs.version }}.yaml" ]; then
-            echo "Error: Release file was not created"
+          if [ ! -d "changelog/releases/${{ github.event.inputs.version }}" ]; then
+            echo "Error: Release directory was not created"
             exit 1
           fi
-          echo "Release file created successfully:"
-          cat "changelog/releases/${{ github.event.inputs.version }}.yaml"
+          echo "Release created successfully:"
+          cat "changelog/releases/${{ github.event.inputs.version }}/manifest.yaml"
+          echo ""
+          echo "Entries:"
+          ls -la "changelog/releases/${{ github.event.inputs.version }}/entries/"
 
+          echo ""
           echo "Updated version.json:"
           cat "version.json"
 
@@ -180,42 +194,24 @@ jobs:
       - name: Generate SBOM artifact
         run: nix run .#generate-sbom -- /tmp/tenzir.spdx.json
 
-      - name: Copy release file to main branch (non-latest release)
+      - name: Copy release to main branch (non-latest release)
         if: steps.release_type.outputs.is_latest == 'false'
         env:
           GITHUB_APP_TOKEN: ${{ steps.generate_token.outputs.token }}
           GH_TOKEN: ${{ steps.generate_token.outputs.token }}
         run: |
-          # Store the release file content
-          cp "changelog/releases/${{ github.event.inputs.version }}.yaml" "/tmp/release.yaml"
-
-          # Extract changelog entry IDs from the release file
-          CHANGE_IDS=$(yq eval '.changes[]' "changelog/releases/${{ github.event.inputs.version }}.yaml")
-
-          # Copy referenced changelog entries to temp directory
-          mkdir -p "/tmp/changes"
-          for CHANGE_ID in $CHANGE_IDS; do
-            if [ -f "changelog/changes/${CHANGE_ID}.md" ]; then
-              cp "changelog/changes/${CHANGE_ID}.md" "/tmp/changes/"
-            fi
-          done
+          # Copy the entire release directory to temp
+          cp -r "changelog/releases/${{ github.event.inputs.version }}" "/tmp/release-dir"
 
           # Switch to main branch
           git fetch origin main
           git checkout main
 
-          # Copy the release file
-          cp "/tmp/release.yaml" "changelog/releases/${{ github.event.inputs.version }}.yaml"
-
-          # Copy the referenced changelog entries
-          for CHANGE_ID in $CHANGE_IDS; do
-            if [ -f "/tmp/changes/${CHANGE_ID}.md" ]; then
-              cp "/tmp/changes/${CHANGE_ID}.md" "changelog/changes/"
-            fi
-          done
+          # Copy the release directory
+          cp -r "/tmp/release-dir" "changelog/releases/${{ github.event.inputs.version }}"
 
           # Commit to main
-          git add "changelog/releases/${{ github.event.inputs.version }}.yaml" changelog/changes/
+          git add "changelog/releases/${{ github.event.inputs.version }}"
           git commit -s -m "Add backport release Tenzir Node ${{ github.event.inputs.version }}"
           git push origin main
 
@@ -260,7 +256,7 @@ jobs:
         if: steps.release_type.outputs.is_latest == 'true'
         run: |
           echo "🎉 Release ${{ github.event.inputs.version }} created successfully!"
-          echo "📁 Release file: changelog/releases/${{ github.event.inputs.version }}.yaml"
+          echo "📁 Release directory: changelog/releases/${{ github.event.inputs.version }}/"
           echo "🏷️ Tag: ${{ github.event.inputs.version }}"
           echo "🌿 Latest branch updated"
           echo "📋 GitHub release: https://github.com/${{ github.repository }}/releases/tag/${{ github.event.inputs.version }}"
@@ -269,5 +265,5 @@ jobs:
         if: steps.release_type.outputs.is_latest == 'false'
         run: |
           echo "🎉 Release ${{ github.event.inputs.version }} created successfully!"
-          echo "📁 Release file: changelog/releases/${{ github.event.inputs.version }}.yaml"
-          echo "📋 Release file copied to main branch"
+          echo "📁 Release directory: changelog/releases/${{ github.event.inputs.version }}/"
+          echo "📋 Release copied to main branch"

@@ -1,3 +1,4 @@
 created: 2025-07-07
+title: "OCSF Trim & Derive"
 intro: |
   This release introduces two new powerful OCSF operators that automate enum derivation and provide intelligent field trimming. The update also includes string padding functions, better HTTP requests, IP categorization and much more!

@@ -1,3 +1,4 @@
 created: 2025-07-23
+title: "Better Performance"
 intro: |
   This release delivers significant performance improvements for situations with many concurrent pipelines, making Tenzir more robust under high-load scenarios. New features include AWS role assumption support, enhanced string trimming functionality, and improved HTTP error handling capabilities. Additionally, this release adds several new operators and comes with various bug fixes.

@@ -1,3 +1,4 @@
 created: 2025-08-01
+title: "HTTP Fix and Compression for Azure Log Analytics"
 intro: |
   This release introduces payload compression for Azure Log Analytics to reduce bandwidth usage, as well as an important fix for a `from_http` bug that was introduced with the previous release.

@@ -1,3 +1,4 @@
 created: 2025-08-02
+title: "OCSF 1.6.0"
 intro: |
   This release adds support for OCSF 1.6.0 and introduces the `replace` operator.

@@ -1,3 +1,4 @@
 created: 2025-08-05
+title: "SecOps & SecurityLake Fixes"
 intro: |
   We fixed two bugs in the `to_google_secops` and `to_amazon_security_lake` operators.

@@ -1,3 +1,4 @@
 created: 2025-08-08
+title: "Enhanced UDP Source"
 intro: |
   This release enhances UDP ingestion with the new `from_udp` operator that produces structured events with sender metadata. We also improved the execution model for `every` and `cron` subpipelines, added DNS lookup capabilities, and made the Syslog parser more flexible.

@@ -1,3 +1,4 @@
 created: 2025-08-21
+title: "Azure Blob Storage & Stability Improvements"
 intro: |
   This release adds a new Azure Blob Storage operator with account key authentication and improves Google Security Operations retry handling. It also contains various small fixes and improvements.

@@ -1,3 +1,4 @@
 created: 2025-08-28
+title: "New S3 Operator & Fork Operator Bugfix"
 intro: |
   This release adds a new S3 operator and fixes a bug within the `fork` operator.

@@ -1,3 +1,4 @@
 created: 2025-09-02
+title: "SentinelOne Data Lake"
 intro: |
   This release introduces an integration fo SentinelOne Singularity™ Data Lake and a new message based `to_kafka` operator that features a one to one event to message relation.

@@ -1,3 +1,4 @@
 created: 2025-09-16
+title: "Lambda Captures"
 intro: |
   This release enhances TQL's data transformation capabilities with lambda expressions that can capture surrounding fields in `map` and `where` functions, plus grouped enumeration for separate event counting. We've also improved operator composability with enhanced `to_splunk` parameters, added octet counting support for syslog messages, and fixed critical issues in Kafka message handling and HTTP request processing.

@@ -1,3 +1,4 @@
 created: 2025-09-22
+title: "Stability Improvements"
 intro: |
   This release brings forth stability improvements under high load that could cause platform unresponsiveness, fixes API request isolation problems, better kafka diagnostics and more.

@@ -1,3 +1,4 @@
 created: 2025-10-01
+title: "Package UDOs"
 intro: |
   This release introduces user-defined operators in packages, allowing you to extend Tenzir with custom operators defined in TQL files. It also adds list manipulation functions, a recursive search function, and improved memory management.

@@ -1,3 +1,4 @@
 created: 2025-10-16
+title: "Performance Improvements"
 intro: |
   This release focuses on improving performance and memory usage. Pipelines are now faster, especially when using if conditions or parsing highly heterogeneous events. Memory usage has also been substantially reduced.

@@ -1,3 +1,4 @@
 created: 2025-10-21
+title: "OCSF Casting & Recursion"
 intro: |
   This release introduces the `ocsf::cast` operator to streamline schema transformations for OCSF events and adds support for one-level recursion in OCSF objects, enabling recursive relations such as `process.parent_process` and `analytic.related_analytics`.

@@ -1,3 +1,4 @@
 created: 2025-10-29
+title: "Better Memory Allocation"
 intro: |
   We continue our quest for better memory usage by switching out the memory allocator to the battle-tested `mimalloc`, as well as adding metrics collecting for memory usage.

@@ -1,3 +1,4 @@
 created: 2025-11-12
+title: "More Memory Metrics"
 intro: |
   This patch release comes with a few new experimental memory metrics. Furthermore, it resolves an issue where the memory usage would grow without bounds on some systems.

@@ -1,3 +1,4 @@
 created: 2025-11-19
+title: "Backport Release"
 intro: |
   This release backports the changes made in v5.21.1 to v5.20.1.

@@ -1,3 +1,4 @@
 created: 2025-11-14
+title: "Better Backpressure"
 intro: |
   This release improves the stability of pipelines by applying backpressure more effectively, ensuring upstream components slow down before overwhelming subscribers. It also enhances daily operations by improving parquet reliability, adding Base58 support, and extending the built-in OCSF operators and functions to support the newly released OCSF 1.7.0.

@@ -1,3 +1,4 @@
 created: 2025-11-20
+title: "New Hash Functions"
 intro: |
   This release features new and improved hash functions as well as a couple of bugfixes.

@@ -1,3 +1,4 @@
 created: 2025-11-28
+title: "Operator Fixups"
 intro: |
   This is a bugfix release that fixes timestamp handling in the python operator and the backpressure handling in publish and subscribe.

@@ -1,3 +1,4 @@
 created: 2025-10-27
+title: "UDO Arguments"
 intro: |
   This release introduces support for arguments in user-defined operators, letting operators declare positional and named parameters with optional default values and use them just like built-in operators. It also enhances parser behavior for duplicate keys and includes several important stability, parsing, and retention improvements to make pipelines more flexible and reliable.

@@ -1,3 +1,4 @@
 created: 2025-05-23
+title: "Talking HTTP"
 intro: |
   This release brings forth improvements to HTTP support in Tenzir, supporting requests as transformations and paginating APIs.

@@ -1,3 +1,4 @@
 created: 2025-06-03
+title: "Fix Python Operator"
 intro: |
   Tenzir Node v5.3.1 updated the pyproject version but did not actually commit it, causing the Python operator to fail to start. This release fixes the issue.

@@ -1,3 +1,4 @@
 created: 2025-06-06
+title: "HTTP fixes"
 intro: |
   The from_http and http operators now support response sizes upto 2GiB

@@ -1,3 +1,4 @@
 created: 2025-06-10
+title: "Fix Demo Node Packages"
 intro: |
   This release fixes a bug that caused package installation outside of the Tenzir Library to fail, which caused Demo Nodes in the Tenzir Platform to not have any packages installed.

@@ -1,3 +1,4 @@
 created: 2025-06-11
+title: "Format Strings"
 intro: |
   With the introduction of format strings to TQL, this release makes string construction from multiple parts easier than ever before.

@@ -1,3 +1,4 @@
 created: 2025-06-13
+title: "JSON Printer Fix"
 intro: |
   This release fixes a bug within the JSON printer that could lead to invalid JSON being produced, and also led to response timeouts when using the Tenzir Platform.

@@ -1,3 +1,4 @@
 created: 2025-06-17
+title: "OCSF Support"
 intro: |
   Built-in support for normalizing OCSF events to their upstream schema makes normalizations easier than ever with Tenzir Node v5.5.

@@ -1,3 +1,4 @@
 created: 2025-06-20
+title: "Dynamic Publish"
 intro: |
   The  operator now supports event-dependent topics, making routing between pipelines more flexible. Additionally, new  and  operators make taking apart custom logs easier than before.

@@ -1,3 +1,4 @@
 created: 2025-06-24
+title: "Fixed Distinct Counts"
 intro: |
   This release restores an aggregation function that was accidentally made unavailable in Tenzir Node v5.6.0.

@@ -1,3 +1,4 @@
 created: 2025-06-25
+title: "Secret Secrets"
 intro: |
   Tenzir Node v5.7.0 introduces a new secret type that keeps its sensitive content hidden while enabling flexible secret retrieval. This release also adds support for OCSF extensions and brings several improvements to the  operator.

@@ -1,3 +1,4 @@
 created: 2025-07-02
+title: "Smarter HTTP Ingestion"
 intro: |
   This release introduces format and compression inference from URLs for HTTP data sources, streamlining data loading workflows. It also includes bug fixes for secret resolution and HTTP server mode.

@@ -1,3 +1,4 @@
 created: 2025-07-05
+title: "UUID Functions"
 intro: |
   This release brings a family of UUID functions to TQL, making it easier to generate random numbers for a variety of use cases.
